items.title: Shipowners Assess Hormuz-Transit Risk as US-Iran Deal Crumbles
items.description: Shipowners painted a mixed picture of their willingness to continue transiting the Strait of Hormuz in the hours after President Donald Trump said the US ceasefire with Iran is “over.”

items.title: Dollar Gets Safe-Haven Flows as US-Iran Ceasefire Under Threat
items.description: The dollar advanced for a second straight session as escalating fears of a return to full-scale war between the US and Iran prompted investors to seek shelter.

items.title: Saudi Efforts to Revive Gulf Oil Loadings Run Into Buyer Caution
items.description: Saudi Arabia’s attempts to revive oil flows from its reopened Ras Tanura terminal on the Persian Gulf are running into caution from customers, who are reluctant to load there because of renewed threat

items.title: Zillow, Rocket to Face August FTC Trial Over Rental-Listing Deal
items.description: Zillow Group Inc. and Rocket Companies Inc.’s Redfin are headed to trial next month in the US Federal Trade Commission’s challenge to an apartment-listing partnership, after a judge in Virginia declin

items.title: Brent’s Surge Runs Into Options Levels That Could Fuel Gains
items.description: Brent crude oil’s 8% gain on Wednesday has futures moving through levels of large open interest that could potentially add fuel to the rally.

items.title: Canadian Banks Too Rich After Searing 66% Run, Jefferies Says
items.description: Canada’s biggest bank stocks are running out of runway as valuations have fully priced in future growth, according to analysts with Jefferies Securities Inc.

items.title: Big Tech’s $25 Billion Mega Bond Sales Are Pushing Market Limits
items.description:
Once a rare event reserved for historic mega-acquisitions, the jumbo bond sale is increasingly becoming the tech sector’s go-to financing tool.
Debt offerings of $25 billion or more have quickly shift

items.title: Stocks Fall as US-Iran Jitters Spur Rally in Oil

items.title: Russia Bans Diesel Exports After Ukraine’s Refinery Attacks
items.description: Russia announced a short-term ban on diesel exports, causing global prices for the fuel to surge to multi-year highs, as a wave of Ukrainian drone attacks leads to domestic shortages.

items.title: ¿Por qué el péndulo de Argentina podría romperse en 2027?
items.description: ¿Y si esta vez se termina el péndulo en Argentina? El mercado ya mira hacia 2027 y empieza a ver señales de que el riesgo político que durante décadas espantó a los inversores podría estar cambiando.

items.description: Once a rare event reserved for historic mega-acquisitions, the jumbo bond sale is increasingly becoming the tech sector’s go-to financing tool.

items.title: Cantor, Adam Back’s BSTR Seek New Terms for Bitcoin SPAC Merger
items.description: A Cantor Fitzgerald-backed blank-check company and Adam Back’s Bitcoin investment company scrapped the original terms of their planned merger and will negotiate a revised deal, as a tougher market for

items.title: Argentina’s Political Parties Look for Ways to Avoid Extremes
items.description: For decades, Argentina’s economic pendulum has whipsawed investors by swinging from one ideological extreme to another, as each new administration dismantled the framework built by its predecessor.

items.title: Dream Finders Homes Boosts Bid for Beazer to $875 Million
items.description: Dream Finders Homes Inc. boosted its takeover offer for rival homebuilder Beazer Homes USA Inc., which said it’s received interest from other potential suitors.

items.title: US Stocks Slide as Oil Jumps After Trump Calls End of Iran Truce
items.description: US equities fell as investors turned risk averse after President Donald Trump said the tentative ceasefire with Iran has ended, fueling concerns of a renewal in fighting.
